title: lappa communis var. major
intro: Lappa communis var. major, also known as the Greater Burdock, is a biennial plant belonging to the Thistle family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds like inulin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been used to treat various health issues. The Greater Burdock is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory problems, wounds, and other health conditions.
